title: Piquant Molten Japanese Cookies Ochazuke
description: A unique dessert featuring molten cookies and tempering chocolate in a traditional Japanese ochazuke form, served in a ceramic tagine vessel. This dish combines bitter and sweet flavors with a monsoon-inspired twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Arrange the matcha cookies in a single layer at the bottom of a ceramic tagine vessel.
Melt the tempering chocolate in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Pour the melted chocolate over the cookies in the tagine vessel.
Sprinkle the toasted sesame seeds and unsweetened coconut flakes over the chocolate.
Drizzle the brewed green tea over the top of the cookie and chocolate mixture.
Sprinkle the kosher salt and monsoon spice blend over the top of the dish.
Dot the top of the dish with unsalted butter.
Bake the ochazuke in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until the cookies are molten and the top is lightly browned.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
